---
title: "iterations and functions"
author: "Radhika"
date: "2026-03-19"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(drc) 
library(tidyverse)

# function

# if F= 32
(5*(32 - 32)/9)

F_to_C <- function(F_degree){
  Celcious<- (5*(F_degree - 32)/9)
  return(Celcious)
}

F_to_C(32)

#sample.function <- function(... variable goes here ...){
 # .... code goes here.... 
#  return(... output ...)}

#iterations

#rep is used for repeating elements
rep("b", 5) # repeats b, 5 times 
rep(c("A", "B"), 5) # repeats A and B, 5 times 
rep(c(1,2,5,2), times = 4, each = 4) # repeats 1 four times, 2 four times, 5 four times, and 2 four times. 

#seq write the sequence 
seq(from = 0, to = 10, by = 2) # sequence of numbers from 0 to 10  by 2s 
seq(from = 1, to = 7) # sequence of numbers 1 to 7

# combined seq() and rep()
rep(seq(from = 0, to = 10, by = 2), times = 3, each = 2)

#SEQ ALONG GIVES THE NUMBER SEQU OF THE LETTERS
LETTERS
seq_along(LETTERS[1:5]) # will return 1,2,3,4,5 not the actual letters.


#LOOP
for(r in 1:10){
  print(r*5)
}

for (i in -30:100){
 result <- F_to_C(i)
 print(result)
}

f<- -30:100
F_to_C(f)

celcius.df <- NULL 
for (i in -30:100){   
result_i <- data.frame(F_to_C(i), i)   
celcius.df <- rbind.data.frame(celcius.df, result_i) 
}
```
